section.title: Overview
section.para: The server-message program is the server counterpart to the messaging client. It uses the message server layer (vrtql_msg_svr), which operates on structured vrtql_msg messages — routing and header maps and a content buffer — rather than raw WebSocket messages. It echoes each message back, preserving its serialisation format.

section.title: The Program
section.para: The complete program follows. It is generated from server-message.c in the companion examples repository.

section.title: Walk-through
section.para: Both the server and message headers are needed, with the support header:
The hook echoes each inbound message. It is declared with the base vws_svr pointer but operates on a vrtql_msg:

The reply mirrors the request's format — keeping the response in the same wire format, JSON or MessagePack — and copies the request's content buffer into the reply's with vws_buffer_append(). The reply is sent through the message server's own send function, reached by casting the base pointer back to its real type: ((vrtql_msg_svr*)s)->send. This matters because the base vws_svr's send expects a vws_msg, whereas the reply here is a vrtql_msg; the message server's send is the one that accepts it. As with the WebSocket server, send takes ownership of the reply, so the handler frees only the incoming message.
The server is created and the handler installed on its hook:

The handler is assigned to process, the message server's per-message hook — again not the internal on_msg_in dispatcher. The server then runs and stops through the base type, exactly as the other servers do:
A vrtql_msg_svr is driven through its base, so the stop-on-signal helper and the blocking vws_tcp_svr_run() both take the server via a (vws_tcp_svr*) cast; vrtql_msg_svr_free() then releases it.

section.title: Building and Running
section.para: The message server needs libvws built with libuv support. Start it, then drive it with the messaging client of :
section.screen: $ ./server-message server-message: vrtql_msg echo server on ws://127.0.0.1:8181/websocket (Ctrl-C to stop)
section.para: Each structured message the client sends is echoed back in the same serialisation format. Press Ctrl-C to stop the server.

section.title: See Also
section.para: The message server is documented in the Server API and Message API sections of the API chapter. Its client counterpart is , and it is layered on the raw-WebSocket server of .
